strategic_profile.executive_summary: The College of William & Mary (W&M) is a premier public research university and the second-oldest institution of higher learning in the United States. Known as a 'Public Ivy,' it blends the highly personalized, teaching-focused environment of a liberal arts college with the rigorous academic and research opportunities of an R1 doctoral university.
strategic_profile.market_position: Public Ivy / Elite Public Research University

analyst_takeaways.insight: W&M has seen a steady increase in applications, maintaining a highly selective overall acceptance rate of 34%, with an early decision acceptance rate approaching 47%.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applying Early Decision provides a significant statistical advantage for students certain that W&M is their top choice.

analyst_takeaways.insight: W&M is consistently ranked among the top universities for undergraduate teaching and happiest students, with strong participation in Greek life and study abroad programs.
analyst_takeaways.implication: The university is an ideal fit for community-oriented students who prioritize faculty mentorship, undergraduate research, and global engagement.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The university meets 100% of demonstrated financial need for in-state students and boasts one of the lowest student loan default rates in the nation at 1%.
analyst_takeaways.implication: W&M offers exceptional return on investment for Virginia residents, though out-of-state students face significantly higher tuition costs.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Highly residential suburban campus with deep historical roots (adjacent to Colonial Williamsburg). Greek life is prominent but not completely dominant (about one-third of undergraduates participate). Known for distinctive campus traditions and high student happiness.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Suburban setting makes it highly walkable, but access to major metropolitan areas requires a vehicle or regional transit.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: Classified as an R1 Doctoral University with very high research activity, heavily emphasizing undergraduate research, including funding through programs like the W&M Scholars Undergraduate Research Experience (WMSURE).

current_status.test_policy_details: Test-optional indefinitely for SAT/ACT. Students may self-report scores on their application. International students whose native language is not English are highly recommended to submit TOEFL or IELTS scores.

gpa.notes: 79% of enrolled students graduated in the top 10% of their high school class.

testing.policy_note: For the Class of 2028, 48% submitted SAT scores and 14% submitted ACT scores.

colleges.name: College of Arts & Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students seeking a rigorous liberal arts curriculum with deep research opportunities.
colleges.housing_profile: Traditional residence halls with high on-campus housing participation.

majors.name: Biology
majors.notes: Grants AP credit for a score of 5 on AP Biology (awards 8 total credits for BIOL 203/204 lecture and lab).

colleges.name: Raymond A. Mason School of Business
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: For students aiming for corporate leadership, finance, consulting, and accounting. Requires strong written communication and early academic performance.

majors.name: Business Administration
majors.special_requirements: Students apply to the Undergraduate Business program in the semester they earn 39 credit hours (usually Fall of sophomore year). Evaluation is holistic, looking at grades, essays, and personal attributes.
majors.notes: Requires a separate application process evaluating academic achievement, personal attributes, and communication skills.

colleges.name: School of Computing, Data Sciences & Physics
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Best for students with strong quantitative skills aiming for careers in tech, software engineering, and data analytics.

colleges.name: School of Education
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students pursuing teaching licensure or educational policy roles.

supplemental_requirements.details: Optional but highly recommended short-answer prompts (max 300 words). Applicants may choose up to two prompts from a list of six topics (e.g., community, academic interests, background/culture, why W&M, overcoming challenges, hometown).

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: W&M actively eliminated legacy preferences for admissions starting with the Fall 2025 cycle to promote equity, and focuses on first-generation and socio-economic diversity.

major_selection_tactics: W&M does not generally admit by major for incoming freshmen in the College of Arts & Sciences, allowing students to apply 'undeclared'.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Because W&M requires an internal application to the Mason School of Business during sophomore year, applying as an undeclared or Arts & Sciences student is standard. Students must ensure they maintain high GPAs and complete prerequisite courses to successfully transfer into the business school.

scholarships.name: 1693 Scholars Program
scholarships.amount: Full in-state tuition, fees, room & board + $5,000
scholarships.benefits: Highly selective (approx. 8 students). Covers full in-state costs and provides a $5,000 research grant. Includes close faculty mentorship and priority registration.

scholarships.name: William & Mary Scholars Award
scholarships.benefits: Awarded to academically distinguished students from diverse backgrounds. Includes participation in the W&M Scholars Undergraduate Research Experience (WMSURE).

scholarships.name: James Monroe Scholars Program
scholarships.benefits: Offered to top 10% of admitted students. Includes a special housing option during the first year and priority course registration.

ap_policy.general_rule: Generally grants credit for scores of 4 or 5 on AP exams.
exceptions: Calculus AB requires a 4 or 5 for MATH 111.
Calculus BC requires a 4 or 5 for MATH 111 and 112, or a 3 for MATH 111.
Biology requires a score of 5 for 8 credits (BIOL 203/204).
ap_policy.usage: Can be applied toward proficiency, minor, major requirements, and Knowledge Domains.

ib_policy.general_rule: Grants credit for scores of 5, 6, or 7 on Higher Level (HL) IB exams.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: AP/IB examinations must have been taken prior to high school graduation or within six months thereafter, but in all cases before entering W&M.

what_it_takes: Extremely rigorous high school coursework (nearly 80% of admits in the top 10% of their class).
Compelling personal essays that show character, resilience, and alignment with W&M's values of community and public service.
A holistic alignment with the university's ethos of intellectual curiosity and collaborative leadership.
essay_tips: Answer the optional supplemental essays (up to two). Doing so shows demonstrated interest and seriousness about applying.
For the 'Community' prompt, it does not have to be an ethnic/racial community—sports teams, clubs, or friend groups are valid.
Show, don't tell. Ensure you place yourself as the main character, highlighting lessons learned and how you'll contribute to the W&M community.
red_flags: Forcing a 'Challenge/Adversity' essay with a weak topic. Only choose this if it shows genuine growth.
Relying on legacy status (legacy preference is completely abolished at W&M).
insights: W&M offers a 'Spring Pathways' program to select waitlisted students, guaranteeing admission for the spring semester if they earn at least 12 transferable credits with a 3.0 GPA elsewhere in the fall.
